Title: Isolation and functional analysis of two CONSTANS-like 1 genes from mango

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MiCOL1A contains four exons and three introns, shares 88% nucleotide and 95% amino acid identity with MiCOL1B, and clusters phylogenetically within CO/COL group IV together with COL1 homologs from Pistacia vera, Citrus sinensis, Eucalyptus grandis and Rosa chinensis. Its native function in mango flowering and drought responses is inferred from expression patterns and conserved activity in Arabidopsis, but has not yet been functionally validated in mango plants.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MiCOL1A is a CONSTANS-LIKE1 homolog from mango with four exons, three introns, and a single CCT domain, grouping into CO/COL family group IV.
2. MiCOL1A is predominantly expressed in juvenile and adult leaves, shows developmentally regulated and circadian expression correlated with floral induction in mango.
3. MiCOL1A protein localizes to the nucleus and exhibits transcriptional activation activity via its front-region domain in yeast.
4. Overexpression of MiCOL1A in Arabidopsis delays flowering under long-day conditions by repressing AtFT and AtSOC1 and increasing AtTFL expression.
5. Overexpression of MiCOL1A enhances drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, promoting root elongation under severe mannitol stress and increasing survival after soil drought, accompanied by upregulation of AtCOR15A, AtNCED3 and AtDREB2.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana delays flowering under long-day conditions and enhances drought tolerance, including increased root elongation under severe mannitol-induced osmotic stress and higher survival after soil drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear-localized transcription factor with intrinsic transcriptional activation activity mediated by its N-terminal front region; in transgenic Arabidopsis, overexpression represses AtFT and AtSOC1 and enhances AtTFL expression, and under drought conditions upregulates stress-responsive genes AtCOR15A, AtNCED3 and AtDREB2.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Photoperiodic flowering pathway, acting as a transcriptional regulator that represses flowering integrator genes FT and SOC1 and upregulates TFL1 homolog expression, and an abiotic stress-response pathway influencing drought-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: MiCOL1A --(transcriptional repression)--> AtFT; MiCOL1A --(transcriptional repression)--> AtSOC1; MiCOL1A --(activation)--> AtTFL; MiCOL1A --(activation under drought)--> AtCOR15A; MiCOL1A --(activation under drought)--> AtNCED3; MiCOL1A --(activation under drought)--> AtDREB2.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CONSTANS-LIKE (CO/COL) family group IV protein containing a single CCT domain and lacking a B-box domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, demonstrated by 35S:MiCOL1A-GFP fusion co-localizing with a nuclear marker in onion epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in all tested mango tissues, with highest levels in juvenile and adult leaves, lower levels in adult stems, and lowest in flowers. Seasonally, expression in adult leaves is lowest during the vegetative stage, increases markedly during floral induction, decreases during floral differentiation, then rises again in late floral differentiation before declining in floral organs. Shows a clear circadian rhythm in leaves, peaking around 3:00 at night and then decreasing.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day photoperiod (16 h light/8 h dark) at approximately 22 °C for Arabidopsis; osmotic stress imposed by 300 and 400 mmol/L mannitol in 1/2 MS medium; soil drought by withholding water for ~12 days followed by 3 days rewatering.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Flowering time regulation and drought stress tolerance in relation to CONSTANS-like transcription factors from mango.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Flowering time; abiotic stress (drought tolerance, osmotic stress).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 400 mmol/L mannitol, primary root length in overexpression lines is approximately 1.6–1.9 cm versus 0.9–1.1 cm in WT. After 12 days of drought and 3 days of rewatering, 85–96% of overexpression plants survive compared with 18–22% of WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression lines exhibit increased plant height compared with WT under long-day conditions; no significant change in rosette leaf number at bolting.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Mangifera indica cv. SiJiMi trees (juvenile and adult leaves, adult stems, flowers) grown in an orchard; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 used to generate 35S:MiCOL1A overexpression lines (T3 homozygous). Onion epidermal cells for transient expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis from mango leaves; PCR cloning and sequencing; exon–intron and conserved domain analysis; phylogenetic analysis of CO/COL proteins; qRT-PCR for spatial, seasonal and circadian expression; PEG-mediated transient transformation of onion epidermal cells and confocal microscopy; yeast transcriptional activation assay using GAL4-BD fusions; Agrobacterium-mediated floral-dip transformation of Arabidopsis; growth under controlled long-day conditions; measurement of days to flowering, rosette leaf number, plant height, primary root length on 1/2 MS with 0, 300, and 400 mmol/L mannitol; soil drought treatment by withholding water for 12 days followed by rewatering; qRT-PCR of flowering- and stress-related Arabidopsis genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and sequence analysis; qRT-PCR for tissue, seasonal and circadian expression in mango; subcellular localization using GFP fusion in onion epidermal cells; yeast-based transcriptional activation assay; constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 under the CaMV35S promoter; phenotyping for flowering time, plant height, root length under mannitol stress, and survival after drought; qRT-PCR of endogenous flowering (AtFT, AtSOC1, AtTFL) and stress-responsive (AtCOR15A, AtNCED3, AtDREB2) genes in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a CONSTANS-LIKE1 homolog from 'SiJiMi' mango transcriptome data and cloned from leaf RNA/cDNA using gene-specific primers and PCR, followed by sequencing and exon–intron structure analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a mango CONSTANS-LIKE1 transcription factor whose overexpression delays flowering and enhances drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, MiCOL1A represents a candidate target for manipulating flowering time and improving drought resistance in mango through marker-assisted selection or genetic engineering once effective strategies for modulating its activity in mango are established.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MiCOL1B contains four exons and three introns, shares 88% nucleotide and 95% amino acid identity with MiCOL1A, and clusters phylogenetically within CO/COL group IV with COL1 homologs from several woody species. Its expression is highest in flowers, contrasting with MiCOL1A, and its circadian expression pattern is less stable across days. Native functional roles in mango flowering and drought responses remain to be experimentally confirmed in mango plants.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MiCOL1B is a CONSTANS-LIKE1 homolog from mango with four exons, three introns, and a single CCT domain, grouping into CO/COL family group IV.
2. MiCOL1B is expressed in all examined mango tissues, with highest expression in flowers and seasonally elevated expression in leaves during floral induction and late floral differentiation, and shows an unstable but circadian-associated diurnal pattern.
3. MiCOL1B protein localizes to the nucleus and displays transcriptional activation activity through its front-region domain in yeast.
4. Overexpression of MiCOL1B in Arabidopsis delays flowering under long-day conditions by repressing AtFT and AtSOC1 and increasing AtTFL expression.
5. Overexpression of MiCOL1B enhances drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, increasing root elongation under severe mannitol stress and survival after soil drought, accompanied by upregulation of AtCOR15A and AtNCED3, while AtDREB2 expression is not consistently significantly elevated.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana delays flowering under long-day conditions and enhances drought tolerance, including increased root elongation under severe mannitol-induced osmotic stress and higher survival after soil drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Nuclear-localized transcription factor with intrinsic transcriptional activation activity via its front-region domain; in transgenic Arabidopsis, overexpression represses AtFT and AtSOC1 and enhances AtTFL expression, and under drought conditions upregulates AtCOR15A and AtNCED3, with AtDREB2 induction not consistently significant in MiCOL1B lines.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Photoperiodic flowering pathway, acting as a transcriptional regulator that represses flowering integrator genes FT and SOC1 and upregulates TFL1 homolog expression, and an abiotic stress-response pathway influencing drought-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: MiCOL1B --(transcriptional repression)--> AtFT; MiCOL1B --(transcriptional repression)--> AtSOC1; MiCOL1B --(activation)--> AtTFL; MiCOL1B --(activation under drought)--> AtCOR15A; MiCOL1B --(activation under drought)--> AtNCED3; MiCOL1B --(no significant activation reported)--> AtDREB2.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CONSTANS-LIKE (CO/COL) family group IV protein containing a single CCT domain and lacking a B-box domain.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, demonstrated by 35S:MiCOL1B-GFP fusion co-localizing with a nuclear marker in onion epidermal cells.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in all tested mango tissues, with highest levels in flowers and lower but similar levels in juvenile leaves, adult leaves and adult stems. Seasonally, expression in adult leaves is lowest during the vegetative stage, increases during floral induction, decreases during floral differentiation, then peaks again in late floral differentiation before declining in floral organs. Circadian analysis shows an unstable diurnal pattern in leaves, with peaks around 0:00 on the first two days and around 18:00 on the third day.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day photoperiod (16 h light/8 h dark) at approximately 22 °C for Arabidopsis; osmotic stress imposed by 300 and 400 mmol/L mannitol in 1/2 MS medium; soil drought by withholding water for ~12 days followed by 3 days rewatering.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Flowering time regulation and drought stress tolerance in relation to CONSTANS-like transcription factors from mango.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Flowering time; abiotic stress (drought tolerance, osmotic stress).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 400 mmol/L mannitol, primary root length in overexpression lines is approximately 1.6–1.9 cm versus 0.9–1.1 cm in WT. After 12 days of drought and 3 days of rewatering, 85–96% of overexpression plants survive compared with 18–22% of WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression lines exhibit increased plant height compared with WT under long-day conditions; no significant change in rosette leaf number at bolting.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Mangifera indica cv. SiJiMi trees (juvenile and adult leaves, adult stems, flowers) grown in an orchard; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 used to generate 35S:MiCOL1B overexpression lines (T3 homozygous). Onion epidermal cells for transient expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA extraction and cDNA synthesis from mango leaves; PCR cloning and sequencing; exon–intron and conserved domain analysis; phylogenetic analysis of CO/COL proteins; qRT-PCR for spatial, seasonal and circadian expression; PEG-mediated transient transformation of onion epidermal cells and confocal microscopy; yeast transcriptional activation assay using GAL4-BD fusions; Agrobacterium-mediated floral-dip transformation of Arabidopsis; growth under controlled long-day conditions; measurement of days to flowering, rosette leaf number, plant height, primary root length on 1/2 MS with 0, 300, and 400 mmol/L mannitol; soil drought treatment by withholding water for 12 days followed by rewatering; qRT-PCR of flowering- and stress-related Arabidopsis genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and sequence analysis; qRT-PCR for tissue, seasonal and circadian expression in mango; subcellular localization using GFP fusion in onion epidermal cells; yeast-based transcriptional activation assay; constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 under the CaMV35S promoter; phenotyping for flowering time, plant height, root length under mannitol stress, and survival after drought; qRT-PCR of endogenous flowering (AtFT, AtSOC1, AtTFL) and stress-responsive (AtCOR15A, AtNCED3, AtDREB2) genes in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a second CONSTANS-LIKE1 homolog from 'SiJiMi' mango transcriptome data and cloned from leaf RNA/cDNA using gene-specific primers and PCR, followed by sequencing and exon–intron structure analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a mango CONSTANS-LIKE1 transcription factor whose overexpression delays flowering and enhances drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, MiCOL1B represents a candidate target for modifying flowering time and improving drought resistance in mango breeding or transgenic approaches, pending direct functional validation in mango.
